title: Auto-repair exact bindings only; keep test pins immutable; surface repairs as escalations
context: Policy bindings become stale when source files are renamed. The system must decide which bindings can safely auto-repair and which require human intervention. Touches invariant(s): con_e04226bd05 (The MCP server must stay client-agnostic: all assistants point to the same .hunch/ graph; no Claude-only behavior in the server).
decision: Repair only exact-identity bindings (exact paths, symbol:file:name selectors) when renamed; skip globs and bare selectors (ambiguous identity); NEVER repair executable-behavior test pins (commit-hash-pinned; repairing would silently break the pin); surface repaired policies as inline escalations requiring re-proof, not silent changes.
consequences: Policies heal automatically for straightforward renames, reducing manual overhead
Test pins stay commit-pinned; renamed tests require re-attestation (human act), preserving proof integrity
Repaired policies surface immediately as escalations; humans are aware of all auto-repairs, not discovering stale proofs later
Proofs become stale by construction (old proof invalid for renamed bindings); re-proof is mandatory before blocking policies can enforce again
Authority/blocking guarantees stay fail-safe: proof revalidation catches moved semantics
alternatives_rejected: Aggressive auto-repair: repair globs and test pins too (risk: silent breakage of hash-pinned tests, scope creep)
Silent repair: auto-heal without surfacing to humans (risk: stale proofs hidden from view, inconsistent mental model)
Inline proof revalidation: auto-update proofs after repair (risk: bypasses the evidence/re-attestation boundary)
Async repair queue: defer to later manual review (risk: inconsistent state, unclear when/if repairs occurred)

rejected_tripwires.alternative: Aggressive auto-repair: repair globs and test pins too (risk: silent breakage of hash-pinned tests, scope creep)
Silent repair: auto-heal without surfacing to humans (risk: stale proofs hidden from view, inconsistent mental model)
Inline proof revalidation: auto-update proofs after repair (risk: bypasses the evidence/re-attestation boundary)
Async repair queue: defer to later manual review (risk: inconsistent state, unclear when/if repairs occurred)
